matches.x: [A Psalm] of David. Bless the LORD, O my soul: and all that is within me, [bless] his holy name.
TO the chief Musician, [A Psalm] of David. In the LORD put I my trust: how say ye to my soul, Flee [as] a bird to your mountain?
A Psalm [and] Song [at] the dedication of the house of David. I will extol thee, O LORD; for thou hast lifted me up, and hast not made my foes to rejoice over me.

And as Enoch saw this, he had bitterness of soul, and wept over his brethren, and said unto the heavens: I will refuse to be comforted; but the Lord said unto Enoch: Lift up your heart, and be glad;…
Rejoice the soul of thy servant: for unto thee, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.

But, behold, it mattereth not--we trust God will deliver us, notwithstanding the weakness of our armies, yea, and deliver us out of the hands of our enemies.
But mine eyes [are] unto thee, O GOD the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul
But as we were allowed of God to be put in trust with the gospel, even so we speak; not as pleasing men, but God, which trieth our hearts.
According to the glorious gospel of the blessed God, which was committed to my trust.
For therefore we both labour and suffer reproach, because we trust in the living God, who is the Saviour of all men, specially of those that believe.
Charge them that are rich in this world, that they be not highminded, nor trust in uncertain riches, but in the living God, who giveth us richly all things to enjoy;
For I was ashamed to require of the king a band of soldiers and horsemen to help us against the enemy in the way: because we had spoken unto the king, saying, The hand of our God [is] upon all them…
Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return [and] be ashamed suddenly.
Yet if [any man suffer] as a Christian, let him not be ashamed; but let him glorify God on this behalf.
And he settled his countenance stedfastly, until he was ashamed: and the man of God wept.
For the Lord God will help me, therefore shall I not be confounded. Therefore have I set my face like a flint, and I know that I shall not be ashamed.
Be not thou therefore ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me his prisoner: but be thou partaker of the afflictions of the gospel according to the power of God;
So they came up to Baalperazim; and David smote them there. Then David said, God hath broken in upon mine enemies by mine hand like the breaking forth of waters: therefore they called the name of…
For he said: Thou art merciful, O God, for thou hast heard my prayer, even when I was in the wilderness; yea, thou wast merciful when I prayed concerning those who were mine enemies, and thou didst…
But, behold, it mattereth not--we trust God will deliver us, notwithstanding the weakness of our armies, yea, and deliver us out of the hands of our enemies.
Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies [upon] the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
[As] with a sword in my bones, mine enemies reproach me; while they say daily unto me, Where [is] thy God?
When I cry [unto thee], then shall mine enemies turn back: this I know; for God [is] for me.
By this I know that thou favourest me, because mine enemy doth not triumph over me.
Now thanks [be] unto God, which always causeth us to triumph in Christ, and maketh manifest the savour of his knowledge by us in every place.
And then, if thou endure it well, God shall exalt thee on high; thou shalt triumph over all thy foes.
Be diligent in keeping all my commandments, lest judgments come upon you, and your faith fail you, and your enemies triumph over you. So no more at present. Amen and Amen.
Save us, O LORD our God, and gather us from among the heathen, to give thanks unto thy holy name, [and] to triumph in thy praise.
TO the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.

And ye shall know that I [am] in the midst of Israel, and [that] I [am] the LORD your God, and none else: and my people shall never be ashamed.

not be ashamed that wait for me. Those who wait for the Lord are those who trust patiently in his plan. Their faith will be vindicated; they will not be ashamed that they believed, because all God's promises are fulfilled. The psalmist said, "O my God, I trust in thee: . . . let none that wait on thee be ashamed" (Ps. 25:2-3). (Donald W. Parry, Jay A. Parry, and Tina M. Peterson, Understanding…
And there are many that lie in wait to destroy thee from off the face of the earth; and for this cause, that thy days may be prolonged, I have given unto thee these commandments.

The good [man] is perished out of the earth: and [there is] none upright among men: they all lie in wait for blood; they hunt every man his brother with a net.

And Elijah said, [As] the LORD of hosts liveth, before whom I stand, I will surely shew myself unto him to day.

The LORD do so and much more to Jonathan: but if it please my father [to do] thee evil, then I will shew it thee, and send thee away, that thou mayest go in peace: and the LORD be with thee, as he…

O then, my beloved brethren, come unto the Lord, the Holy One. Remember that his paths are righteous. Behold, the way for man is narrow, but it lieth in a straight course before him, and the keeper…
Teach me to do thy will; for thou [art] my God: thy spirit [is] good; lead me into the land of uprightness.
For if ye turn again unto the LORD, your brethren and your children [shall find] compassion before them that lead them captive, so that they shall come again into this land: for the LORD your God…

[So] the LORD alone did lead him, and [there was] no strange god with him.
Be thou humble; and the Lord thy God shall lead thee by the hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers.
And when they were come, they say unto him, Master, we know that thou art true, and carest for no man: for thou regardest not the person of men, but teachest the way of God in truth: Is it lawful to…

But as for me, my prayer [is] unto thee, O LORD, [in] an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.
He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
And thus are the secrets of his heart made manifest; and so falling down on [his] face he will worship God, and report that God is in you of a truth.
But the anointing which ye have received of him abideth in you, and ye need not that any man teach you: but as the same anointing teacheth you of all things, and is truth, and is no lie, and even as…

And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this [is] our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this [is] the LORD; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
Therefore I will look unto the LORD; I will wait for the God of my salvation: my God will hear me.
But as for me, my prayer [is] unto thee, O LORD, [in] an acceptable time: O God, in the multitude of thy mercy hear me, in the truth of thy salvation.
He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God.
And say ye, Save us, O God of our salvation, and gather us together, and deliver us from the heathen, that we may give thanks to thy holy name, [and] glory in thy praise.
And then at that day will they not rejoice and give praise unto their everlasting God, their rock and their salvation? Yea, at that day, will they not receive the strength and nourishment from the…
Therefore I will look unto the LORD; I will wait for the God of my salvation: my God will hear me.
But as for us, the LORD [is] our God, and we have not forsaken him; and the priests, which minister unto the LORD, [are] the sons of Aaron, and the Levites [wait] upon [their] business:
My righteousness is near; my salvation is gone forth, and mine arm shall judge the people. The isles shall wait upon me, and on mine arm shall they trust.
And now we only wait to hear the joyful news declared unto us by the mouth of angels, of his coming; for the time cometh, we know not how soon. Would to God that it might be in my day; but let it be…
And if a man lie not in wait, but God deliver [him] into his hand; then I will appoint thee a place whither he shall flee.
Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
O LORD God, turn not away the face of thine anointed: remember the mercies of David thy servant.

O LORD, I have heard thy speech, [and] was afraid: O LORD, revive thy work in the midst of the years, in the midst of the years make known; in wrath remember mercy.
8. O remember not our former iniquities! Let thy tender mercies come quickly to us!

And after I had traveled for the space of many hours in darkness, I began to pray unto the Lord that he would have mercy on me, according to the multitude of his tender mercies.
Behold, we count them happy which endure. Ye have heard of the patience of Job, and have seen the end of the Lord; that the Lord is very pitiful, and of tender mercy.
Great [are] thy tender mercies, O LORD: quicken me according to thy judgments.
8. O remember not our former iniquities! Let thy tender mercies come quickly to us!

O give thanks unto the LORD; for [he is] good; for his mercy [endureth] for ever.

I will remember the works of the LORD: surely I will remember thy wonders of old.
But David took not the number of them from twenty years old and under: because the LORD had said he would increase Israel like to the stars of the heavens.
For it came to pass, when Solomon was old, [that] his wives turned away his heart after other gods: and his heart was not perfect with the LORD his God, as [was] the heart of David his father.

And thou shalt see an enemy [in my] habitation, in all [the wealth] which [God] shall give Israel: and there shall not be an old man in thine house for ever.

I, [even] I, [am] he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins.

PSALM: XXV. David's prayer for remission of sins and for help in affliction. A Psalm of David. NTO thee, 0 Lord, do I lift up my soul,
And the LORD smelled a sweet savour; and the LORD said in his heart, I will not again curse the ground any more for man's sake; for the imagination of man's heart [is] evil from his youth; neither…
Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith the LORD; I remember thee, the kindness of thy youth, the love of thine espousals, when thou wentest after me in the wilderness, in a land…
Fear not, for thou shalt not be ashamed; neither be thou confounded, for thou shalt not be put to shame; for thou shalt forget the shame of thy youth, and shalt not remember the reproach of thy…
For the Lord hath called thee as a woman forsaken and grieved in spirit, and a wife of youth, when thou wast refused, saith thy God.
O, remember, my son, and learn wisdom in thy youth; yea, learn in thy youth to keep the commandments of God.

I, [even] I, [am] he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins.

But do thou for me, O GOD the Lord, for thy name's sake: because thy mercy [is] good, deliver thou me.
O give thanks unto the LORD; for [he is] good; for his mercy [endureth] for ever.
And after I had traveled for the space of many hours in darkness, I began to pray unto the Lord that he would have mercy on me, according to the multitude of his tender mercies.

"Speak to the children of Israel, saying... When you cross over... and you shall designate cities for yourselves." This is what the verse means: "Good and upright is the Lord; therefore, He instructs sinners on the way" (Psalms 25:8). David said before the Lord: "Master of the Universe, if not for Your mercy, Adam, the first man, would not have had a standing. When You said to him, 'On the day…

What is the secret of the Holy One. This is circumcision, since the Holy One revealed the mystery100Gk.: mysterion. of circumcision to no one but…

It is written: “The secret of the Lord is [revealed] to those who fear Him, and His covenant to inform them” (Psalms 25:14). What is “the secret of the Lord”? This is circumcision,7Often referred to as brit, the covenant. as He did not reveal it from Adam the first man until twenty generations, until Abraham arose, and He gave it to him, as it is stated: “I will establish My covenant between Me…

[2] Another interpretation: "And I will establish My covenant" (Genesis 17:2). This refers to the secret of God for those who fear Him, and His covenant to make it known to them (Psalms 25:14). What is the secret of the Holy One, blessed be He? It is a word that the Holy One, blessed be He, did not reveal, a mystery of a word that passed from person to person for twenty generations, until Abraham…

RESH. Consider mine affliction, and deliver me: for I do not forget thy law.
Consider [and] hear me, O LORD my God: lighten mine eyes, lest I sleep the [sleep of] death;
Have mercy upon me, O LORD; consider my trouble [which I suffer] of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:
I hate them with perfect hatred: I count them mine enemies.
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies, that I might destroy them that hate me.
If I whet my glittering sword, and mine hand take hold on judgment; I will render vengeance to mine enemies, and will reward them that hate me.
And my presence shall be with you even in avenging me of mine enemies, unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me.
Thou hast also given me the necks of mine enemies; that I might destroy them that hate me.
Let not them that are mine enemies wrongfully rejoice over me: [neither] let them wink with the eye that hate me without a cause.

PSALM: XXV. David's prayer for remission of sins and for help in affliction. A Psalm of David. NTO thee, 0 Lord, do I lift up my soul,
TO the chief Musician, A Psalm of David. In thee, O LORD, do I put my trust; let me never be ashamed: deliver me in thy righteousness.
Now therefore, O king, come down according to all the desire of thy soul to come down; and our part [shall be] to deliver him into the king's hand.
